title: Complex Numbers
summary: This page illustrates the conversion from polar to Cartesian form of complex numbers through multiple figures (Figure B.5). It then introduces arithmetical operations on complex numbers, demonstrating that addition and subtraction are most conveniently performed in Cartesian form, with an example using z1 = 3 + j4 and z2 = 2 + j3.

figures.caption: Graphical representations on the complex plane illustrating the conversion of complex numbers from polar (exponential) form to Cartesian form for various examples including positive and negative angles and multiple full rotations.
